vitals.location: St. Georgen im Attergau, Austria (CHALLENGE category route — the flagship Extreme race covers ~2,200km/30,000m around all of Austria)
vitals.date_specific: 2026: August 10-16 (Extreme starts Aug 10-11; other divisions through Aug 12; finishes to Aug 16)
route_options: CHALLENGE: 560 km / ~6,500m (this profile's vitals) — supported, unsupported, and 2-person team categories
Extreme (flagship): ~2,200 km / 30,000m nonstop, solo or 2-/4-person relay (2026 WUCA World Championship course: 2,160km)

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 69. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 64. FLAG (2026-07-24 editorial wave 7): this is a self-supported/supported ultracycling event (24hr-format CHALLENGE category, 560km/~6,500m), not a conventional gran_fondo — every fondo dimension here (descent_technicality, scenic_experience, community_culture, etc.) was scored against ultra-format assumptions that may not map cleanly. Also, founded year corrected 2011->2009. Recommend a human re-check the discipline/scoring fit (see catalog_flags taxonomy note). Numbers left untouched per no-silent-reshuffle rule.

course_description.character: Race Around Austria is a family of ultracycling time trials based in St. Georgen im Attergau, Upper Austria. This profile's stored vitals (560km/~6,500m) describe the CHALLENGE category — a single loop around Upper Austria run in a 24-hour-race format, with both supported and unsupported solo options plus team categories. The flagship 'Extreme' race is a much bigger undertaking: a supported, nonstop individual time trial of roughly 2,200 km/30,000m around the entire country through eight Austrian states, including the Styrian wine roads, Großglockner, western Alpine passes, the Inn Valley and Salzburg approaches — with no stages and a clock that never stops. A 1,500 km/~17,500m 'RAA 1500' sits between the two. The 2025 and 2026 WUCA World Championships run on the Extreme course (2,160 km, supported mode).
course_description.signature_challenge: For the CHALLENGE category this profile describes: a single 560km/~6,500m nonstop loop around Upper Austria within a 24-hour format. For the flagship Extreme race (a distinct, much longer event): the Großglockner and western Alpine passes deep into a nonstop, multi-day national circumnavigation.
suffering_zones: Overnight riding on Upper Austria back roads — sleep deprivation is a real factor even at the 560km CHALLENGE distance
The rolling return leg back into St. Georgen im Attergau, ridden on depleted legs late in the 24-hour window

logistics.transport: Start/finish: St. Georgen im Attergau, Upper Austria.

history.origin_story: First official Race Around Austria held in 2009 (not 2011). The RAA 1500 was added in 2013, the CHALLENGE category in 2014, and the Unsupported Challenge option in 2020.
notable_moments: 2025/2026: WUCA World Championships integrated into the Extreme course

biased_opinion.verdict: Legitimate Ultra Series, But Not a Gran Fondo
biased_opinion.summary: Race Around Austria is a genuine, well-organized national ultracycling institution — first run in 2009, now hosting the WUCA World Championships on its flagship ~2,200km/30,000m Extreme course, with a 560km/~6,500m CHALLENGE category (this profile's stored distance) and a 1,500km RAA 1500 in between. It is fundamentally an ultracycling time trial festival, not a conventional single-day gran fondo, and its presence in this database under gran_fondo scoring dimensions is a taxonomy mismatch worth flagging rather than silently correcting. Whichever distance you enter, expect extraordinary crew, vehicle and travel burden well beyond what a headline entry fee suggests.
strengths: Genuine national institution with real WUCA World Championship status on the flagship Extreme course
Well-organized, established event with a real history back to 2009 and a clear progression of distances (CHALLENGE 560km, RAA 1500, Extreme ~2,200km)
The CHALLENGE category offers supported, unsupported, and team options, giving a real entry point below the flagship Extreme distance
Genuine Alpine and cross-country Austrian scenery on the longer courses
weaknesses: Not a gran fondo despite this catalog's discipline tag — no stages, no closed course, no mass-participation single-day format

Extraordinarily high real-world cost on supported categories — crew, support vehicle, and travel expenses dwarf the headline entry fee (the Unsupported Challenge avoids this, but trades it for reduced intervention capacity)
Sleep deprivation, open traffic, and variable Alpine weather make this qualitatively different from any fondo on this list
This profile's stored 560km/~6,500m vitals describe only the CHALLENGE category, not the ~2,200km flagship Extreme race most people mean by 'Race Around Austria'
biased_opinion.bottom_line: A legitimate, well-run ultracycling event worth respecting on its own terms — but score and market it as an ultra, not a fondo, and be clear about which of its three distances you're actually describing.

final_verdict.one_liner: Legitimate Austrian ultracycling institution (CHALLENGE: 560km/~6,500m; flagship Extreme: ~2,200km/30,000m) — not a conventional gran fondo.
final_verdict.should_you_race: Yes if you're specifically seeking an ultracycling challenge with real WUCA-championship pedigree and can absorb the crew/travel costs. Not a fit if you're looking for a single-day, closed-course mass-participation gran fondo experience.
final_verdict.alternatives: For a comparable European national-circumnavigation ultra, see Race Around Poland (also in this catalog). For a conventional single-day Austrian road gran fondo, look elsewhere in the catalog.

catalog_flags.status_note: Taxonomy note: this is an ultracycling time-trial/festival (multiple distances: 560km CHALLENGE, 1,500km RAA 1500, ~2,200km flagship Extreme), not a conventional gran fondo. This profile's stored vitals describe the CHALLENGE category only. Not changing the discipline enum per editorial-wave scope — flagging for a human taxonomy decision on whether/how to represent the flagship Extreme distance separately.
